evals.name: specific-issue-number
evals.expected_output: Resolves issue #42, reads it with gh issue view, checks for blocking labels, then initializes a koto workflow with `koto init issue_42 --template ... --var ISSUE_NUMBER=42 --var ARTIFACT_PREFIX=issue_42`. Submits entry evidence with mode: issue_backed. Enters the koto execution loop using `koto next issue_42` to advance through states.
expectations: Agent plans to read issue #42 using gh issue view
Agent plans to check for blocking labels before proceeding
Agent plans to run koto init with --template flag and --var ISSUE_NUMBER=42
Agent plans to set ARTIFACT_PREFIX to issue_42 in koto init
Agent plans to submit entry evidence with mode issue_backed
Agent plans to enter the koto execution loop using koto next with the workflow name
Agent plans to detect repo visibility from CLAUDE.md or infers from path

evals.name: milestone-auto-select
evals.expected_output: Lists open issues in milestone 3, identifies dependency relationships, selects the lowest-numbered unblocked issue, then proceeds with the full workflow for that issue. Reports which issue was selected and why.
expectations: Agent plans to recognize M3 as a milestone reference, not an issue number
Agent plans to list open issues in milestone 3 using gh
Agent plans to check dependency sections of milestone issues for blocking
Agent plans to select the lowest-numbered unblocked issue
Agent plans to report which issue was selected and why
Agent plans to proceed to koto init with the selected issue number

evals.name: auto-mode-flag
evals.expected_output: Runs in automatic execution mode. At decision points (W1, W2), follows the decision protocol from references/decision-protocol.md instead of asking the user. Uses `koto decisions record` to capture any decisions made. Safety gates (W3, W4) still block and require user input. Uses koto orchestration throughout.
expectations: Agent plans to detect the --auto flag from the arguments
Agent plans to reference auto execution mode or decision protocol
Agent plans to use koto decisions record for decision capture
Agent plans to use koto orchestration (koto init / koto next)
Agent plans to distinguish between decision points (W1/W2 auto) and safety gates (W3/W4 blocking)
Agent plans to read issue #15 with gh issue view

evals.name: blocking-label-needs-design
evals.expected_output: Reads issue #8 which has a needs-design label. Detects the blocking label from the project's label vocabulary, displays a routing message explaining the issue requires a design document before implementation, and stops execution without initializing a koto workflow.
expectations: Agent reads issue #8 with gh issue view
Agent checks for blocking labels from the project label vocabulary
Agent identifies needs-design as a blocking label
Agent displays a routing message about the blocking label
Agent stops execution without initializing a koto workflow
Agent does NOT attempt koto init

evals.name: needs-triage-label
evals.expected_output: Reads issue #23 which has a needs-triage label. Checks the project's label vocabulary for routing options. If a triage workflow is defined in the extension file, invokes it. Otherwise, asks the user whether to proceed directly or reclassify the issue first.
expectations: Agent reads issue #23 with gh issue view
Agent detects the needs-triage label
Agent checks the project label vocabulary (in CLAUDE.md or extension file)
Agent checks the extension file for a triage workflow definition
Agent either invokes a triage workflow or asks user whether to proceed
Agent invokes the triage workflow rather than implementing the issue directly

evals.name: resume-interrupted-session
evals.expected_output: Checks for an active koto workflow with `koto workflows`. Finds an existing workflow, resumes with `koto next <workflow-name>`.
expectations: Agent checks for active koto workflows using koto workflows
Agent attempts to resume an existing workflow rather than starting fresh
Agent uses koto next with the workflow name to resume
Agent does NOT run koto init if an active workflow is detected
Agent reads the koto next response to determine the current state and what work is needed

evals.name: milestone-all-blocked
evals.expected_output: Lists open issues in milestone 5, checks dependency sections, finds that all issues are blocked by open dependencies. Reports which issues are blocked and by what, then stops without starting any implementation.
expectations: Agent plans to recognize M5 as a milestone reference
Agent plans to list open issues in milestone 5
Agent plans to check dependency sections of each issue
Agent plans to determine that all issues are blocked by open dependencies
Agent plans to report which issues are blocked and by what
Agent plans to stop without starting any implementation or koto workflow

evals.name: issue-url-input
evals.expected_output: Parses the GitHub issue URL to extract issue number 71, reads the issue with gh issue view, checks for blocking labels, detects repo visibility from CLAUDE.md, loads the appropriate content governance skill, and initializes a koto workflow for the issue.
expectations: Agent plans to parse the GitHub URL to extract issue number 71
Agent plans to use gh issue view to read the issue
Agent plans to detect repo visibility from CLAUDE.md or path inference
Agent plans to load the appropriate content governance skill based on visibility
Agent plans to initialize a koto workflow with the extracted issue number
Agent plans to set ARTIFACT_PREFIX to issue_71

evals.name: free-form-mode
evals.prompt: /work-on fix the login timeout -- no issue needed
evals.expected_output: Detects free-form input (no issue number), initializes koto with `koto init task_fix-login-timeout --template ... --var ARTIFACT_PREFIX=task_fix-login-timeout`. Submits entry evidence with mode: free_form and the task description.
expectations: Agent plans to detect free-form input (no issue number or milestone)
Agent plans to use ARTIFACT_PREFIX=task_fix-login-timeout or similar slug
Agent plans to submit entry evidence with mode free_form
Agent plans to include the task description in entry evidence
Agent plans to initialize koto with --template and without ISSUE_NUMBER var
Agent plans to NOT run gh issue view for a non-existent issue

evals.name: koto-not-installed
evals.expected_output: Agent resolves issue #10, reads it successfully, detects koto is not installed when running koto version, then installs koto using the install script before proceeding.
expectations: Agent reads issue #10 with gh issue view
Agent attempts to run koto version to check prerequisites
Agent detects that koto is not installed or not on PATH
Agent attempts to install koto using curl and the install script
Agent does NOT skip koto and fall back to manual orchestration

evals.name: gate-failure-blocks-transition
evals.expected_output: In the implementation state, when has_commits gate returns non-zero exit (no commits on branch yet), the workflow does not advance to finalization. The state requires re-entry after commits are made.
expectations: When the has_commits gate returns non-zero exit code, the workflow does not route to finalization
The implementation state requires gates.has_commits.exit_code to be 0 before transitioning to finalization
Agent retries or reports the gate failure without advancing to finalization
Gate-based routing is respected: evidence alone (implementation_status: complete) is insufficient if gates fail

evals.name: review-panel-passed
evals.expected_output: Agent enters scrutiny state, runs three panel reviewers (completeness, justification, intent) in parallel, receives passed verdicts from all three, then submits scrutiny_outcome: passed to advance to review.
expectations: Agent submits scrutiny_outcome: passed when panel reviewers all approve
Agent does not submit blocking_retry or blocking_escalate when issues are found to be correctable
Agent checks scrutiny_results context-exists gate status before submitting evidence
Agent plans to run all three panel reviewers before submitting scrutiny outcome

evals.name: scrutiny-blocking-retry-entry
evals.expected_output: Scrutiny panel finds blocking issues and emits blocking_retry. Workflow returns to implementation state rather than done_blocked. Agent recognizes the retry path and prepares to address the blocking feedback.
expectations: Agent submits scrutiny_outcome: blocking_retry after panel finds blocking issues
Workflow returns to implementation state (not done_blocked)
Agent recognizes that blocking_retry loops back to implementation, not escalation
Agent does NOT submit blocking_escalate for correctable issues

evals.name: review-panel-escalate
evals.expected_output: Panel emits blocking_escalate. Workflow routes to done_blocked with failure_reason written via context_assignments. Agent does not retry implementation.
expectations: Agent submits scrutiny_outcome: blocking_escalate with failure_reason
Agent does not retry implementation after submitting blocking_escalate outcome
Agent includes failure_reason string in the evidence submitted to koto (required for context_assignments to propagate)
Agent reports the escalation reason to the user

evals.name: plan-backed-github-issue
evals.expected_output: Agent detects plan_backed mode, submits entry with mode: plan_backed and issue_source: github. Routes through plan_context_injection to read the GitHub issue, then proceeds to setup_plan_backed, skips staleness, continues to analysis.
expectations: Agent plans to submit entry evidence with mode: plan_backed
Agent plans to set issue_source: github in entry evidence
Agent plans to route through plan_context_injection state
Agent plans to read GitHub issue via gh issue view for ISSUE_SOURCE=github
Agent plans to proceed to setup_plan_backed after context injection
Agent plans to route to analysis without staleness_check (plan-backed skips staleness)
Agent plans to use status: override in setup_plan_backed when SHARED_BRANCH is provided, committing directly to it instead of creating a new branch

evals.name: plan-backed-outline-item
evals.expected_output: Agent detects plan_backed mode with ISSUE_SOURCE=plan_outline. Routes through plan_context_injection (extracts issue outline from PLAN doc), plan_validation, setup_plan_backed, then analysis. Does not call gh issue view.
expectations: Agent plans to submit entry with mode: plan_backed and issue_source: plan_outline
Agent plans to extract the issue outline from the PLAN doc (not call gh issue view)
Agent plans to route through plan_validation state
Agent plans to skip staleness_check for plan_outline source
Agent does NOT plan to call gh issue view for plan_outline source
Agent plans to use status: override in setup_plan_backed when SHARED_BRANCH is provided, committing directly to it instead of creating a new branch

evals.name: skipped-marker-reached
evals.prompt: /work-on -- scheduler routes child into skipped state due to failed dependency
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes mode: skipped at entry and routes to skipped_due_to_dep_failure terminal. Does not perform any implementation work.
expectations: Agent plans to recognize mode: skipped as a valid entry mode
Agent plans to route to skipped_due_to_dep_failure terminal state
Agent does NOT plan to implement anything when mode is skipped
Agent understands skipped_due_to_dep_failure has skipped_marker: true

evals.name: plan-mode-detection-from-path
evals.expected_output: Agent detects the PLAN.md path and acts as the thin dispatcher: it reads execution_mode from the PLAN frontmatter, re-validates it against the closed set {single-pr, multi-pr, coordinated}, and routes. For this multi-pr PLAN it runs in place -- selects the next unblocked issue and runs it as a single issue-backed unit landing its own PR. A single-pr or coordinated PLAN would instead be handed off to /execute. The dispatcher does NOT initialize a plan-orchestrator template and does NOT run plan-to-tasks.sh; that orchestration now lives in /execute. It does NOT call gh issue view to detect the PLAN path.
expectations: Agent detects the PLAN.md path and routes via the dispatcher (reads execution_mode, not issue-backed or free-form)
Agent re-validates execution_mode against {single-pr, multi-pr, coordinated} before any path or branch interpolation
Agent routes multi-pr in place (selects the next unblocked issue, runs it as a single issue-backed unit landing its own PR)
Agent hands single-pr and coordinated PLANs off to /execute rather than running them in /work-on
Agent does NOT initialize a plan-orchestrator template or run plan-to-tasks.sh (orchestration lives in /execute)

evals.name: e2e-cross-mode-no-contamination
evals.prompt: /work-on -- three sequential invocations in one session: (1) /work-on #42 (2) /work-on "fix the login timeout" (3) /work-on skills/execute/evals/fixtures/plans/PLAN-multi-pr-test.md -- describe the template/dispatch and vars used for each
evals.expected_output: For (1) issue-backed: koto init with work-on.md, ISSUE_NUMBER=42, ARTIFACT_PREFIX=issue_42, mode: issue_backed. For (2) free-form: koto init with work-on.md, ARTIFACT_PREFIX=task_fix-login-timeout, mode: free_form, no ISSUE_NUMBER. For (3) the PLAN path is handled by the dispatcher: read execution_mode and route (multi-pr in place one issue at a time; single-pr/coordinated handed off to /execute) -- no plan-orchestrator template. Each invocation uses distinct dispatch and vars with no state carried from prior invocations.
expectations: For invocation (1) issue-backed, agent plans to use work-on.md template with ISSUE_NUMBER=42 and ARTIFACT_PREFIX=issue_42
For invocation (2) free-form, agent plans to use work-on.md template with ARTIFACT_PREFIX derived from task slug but no ISSUE_NUMBER
For invocation (3) plan path, agent routes via the dispatcher (reads execution_mode; multi-pr in place, single-pr/coordinated handed to /execute) and does NOT initialize a plan-orchestrator template
Agent does not reuse or carry over ISSUE_NUMBER, ARTIFACT_PREFIX, or template from one invocation to the next
Each mode submits correct entry evidence: issue_backed uses mode: issue_backed; free_form uses mode: free_form; the plan path dispatches on execution_mode

evals.name: already-complete-terminal
evals.expected_output: Agent receives the analysis state. Recognizes the issue goal is already fully satisfied by current code and submits plan_outcome: already_complete. Workflow routes to done_already_complete terminal state. Agent does not proceed to implementation. Reports to user that the issue is already resolved.
expectations: Agent submits plan_outcome: already_complete from the analysis state
Agent recognizes done_already_complete as a non-failure terminal state
Agent does NOT submit plan_outcome: plan_ready when the goal is already met
Agent does NOT proceed to implementation after submitting already_complete
Agent reports to the user that the issue is already resolved

evals.name: docs-issue-type-skips-panels
evals.expected_output: Agent receives the implementation state for a documentation issue. Submits implementation_status: complete with issue_type: docs. Workflow routes directly to finalization without visiting scrutiny, review, or qa_validation states.
expectations: Agent submits implementation_status: complete with issue_type: docs
Agent does NOT submit issue_type: code for a documentation issue
Agent understands that issue_type: docs routes to finalization directly, skipping review panels
Agent does NOT attempt to run scrutiny or review panels for a docs issue type
Agent recognizes that tests_passing gate is not required for docs type transitions

evals.name: task-issue-type-skips-panels
evals.expected_output: Agent receives the implementation state for an operational task issue. Submits implementation_status: complete with issue_type: task. Workflow routes directly to finalization without visiting scrutiny, review, or qa_validation. Agent recognizes that task type does not require commits before transitioning.
expectations: Agent submits implementation_status: complete with issue_type: task
Agent does NOT submit issue_type: code for an operational task
Agent understands that issue_type: task routes to finalization directly, skipping review panels
Agent does NOT attempt to run scrutiny or review panels for a task issue type
Agent recognizes that has_commits gate is not required for task type transitions

evals.name: entry-plan-outline-skip
evals.expected_output: Agent receives entry state with advanced: true, indicating koto auto-advanced via skip_if because ISSUE_SOURCE is plan_outline. Agent does not manually submit mode: plan_backed — it calls koto next again to continue to plan_context_injection.
expectations: Agent does NOT submit mode: plan_backed manually for entry when advanced: true
Agent recognizes advanced: true means koto auto-advanced entry via skip_if on ISSUE_SOURCE=plan_outline
Agent calls koto next again without submitting any evidence for entry
Agent continues to plan_context_injection after the auto-advance

evals.name: setup-plan-backed-shared-branch-skip
evals.expected_output: Agent receives setup_plan_backed state with advanced: true, indicating koto auto-advanced via skip_if because SHARED_BRANCH is set. Agent does not manually submit evidence for setup_plan_backed. Agent calls koto next again immediately to continue to analysis.
expectations: Agent does NOT submit status: completed or status: override manually for setup_plan_backed when advanced: true
Agent recognizes advanced: true means koto auto-advanced this state via skip_if
Agent calls koto next again without submitting any evidence for setup_plan_backed
Agent continues to the analysis state after the auto-advance

evals.name: pr-shared-skips-ci-monitor
evals.expected_output: Agent receives the pr_creation state with SHARED_BRANCH set. Recognizes this child is running on the orchestrator's shared branch where the orchestrator owns the PR. Submits pr_status: shared. Workflow routes directly to done without visiting ci_monitor.
expectations: Agent submits pr_status: shared from pr_creation state
Agent does NOT attempt to create a new PR when SHARED_BRANCH is indicated
Agent understands that pr_status: shared routes directly to done, bypassing ci_monitor
Agent does NOT run gh pr create when the orchestrator already owns the PR
Agent recognizes done as the terminal state reached via the shared path

evals.name: dod-verified-by-execution
evals.prompt: /work-on #501 — an issue that changes a shirabe skill (edits skills/foo/SKILL.md). The issue's branch already has the skill's evals authored and committed, but they have not been run this session.
evals.expected_output: The agent's plan reaches the verification state before finalization, reads the project verification map from .claude/shirabe-extensions/work-on.md, sees skills/** maps to scripts/run-evals.sh, and runs that verification, requiring it to pass. The plan explicitly treats authored-but-unrun evals as NOT satisfying the gate (existence is not execution) and does not report the issue done until the verification has run and passed.
expectations: Agent's plan includes a verification step before finalization that runs the project-declared verification for what the issue touched
Agent reads the verification map (.claude/shirabe-extensions/work-on.md) and runs scripts/run-evals.sh for the skills/** change rather than only confirming evals exist
Agent states that authored-but-unrun verification does NOT satisfy the gate (existence is not execution)
Agent does not report the issue done until the verification has actually run and passed

evals.name: dod-fail-closed-cannot-verify
evals.prompt: /work-on #502 — a single issue whose change touches files that match no entry in the project's verification map, in a project that declares no default test command and where no standard test command can be determined.
evals.expected_output: The agent's plan recognizes it cannot determine or run an applicable verification, yields a cannot-verify outcome, and routes to a human decision rather than reporting the issue done. The plan fails closed: it never treats absence of verification as a pass.
expectations: Agent's plan recognizes the cannot-verify condition (no map entry matches and no usable default test command)
Agent fails closed: it routes to a human decision / blocked outcome rather than reporting the issue done
Agent explicitly does NOT treat absence of verification as a passing result (no silent-pass)

evals.name: dod-no-silent-deferral
evals.prompt: /work-on #503 — a single issue with one acceptance criterion that genuinely cannot be satisfied this session (a flaky external dependency). The rest of the issue is complete and verified.
evals.expected_output: The agent's plan does not self-report the issue done with a caveat. It surfaces the unmet acceptance criterion to the human as an explicit decision: approve a deferral (recorded as the human's decision) or report the issue blocked. The plan introduces no unapproved caveat/hedge language into the shipped artifacts.
expectations: Agent's plan surfaces the unmet acceptance criterion to the human as an explicit decision rather than self-reporting done
Agent requires explicit human approval to defer; an approved deferral is recorded as the human's decision
Without approval the agent reports the issue blocked, not done
Agent does not introduce unapproved caveat or hedge language into the shipped artifacts
